snippet.title: Have I found the BEST BUDGET iPhone 17 Pro Max Cases?!📱⚡️ #ESRTech #iPhone17ProMax #iphone17pro
snippet.description:
First thing I do when I get a new iPhone — throw a case and screen protector on it 😂
.Been using ESR stuff (wireless chargers, screen protectors, cases) for a few years now and honestly, it just works. Their cases always feel solid.
.
The new Classic Hybrid lineup looks so clean — the frosted texture feels super nice in hand and the metal stand around the camera bump is actually super useful.
.
If you’re a bit outdoorsy and rough with your gear, the Cyber Tough case is built like a tank. “Poron” foam padding inside, USB port protector, mesh over the speakers, and the same metal stand — and I’m loving the green + orange Master Chief look!
.
Now the Armorite screen protectors deserve their own shoutout.
.A few years back I couldn’t find any screen protectors that didn’t have those sharp edges that catch your fingers. ESR’s were one of the first that actually felt smooth all the way around, and this year’s are just as good.
.They’ve added an applicator this time too — makes it way easier to line up and keeps dust out. The pull tab thing actually uses static to grab any last bits before you drop it on, and it just works. 
.
Plus you get three in the box, which is great if you mess one up.
.
Whenever people ask me what budget-friendly cases or protectors to get — I always say ESR. They’ve never missed.
.
🔗
ESR Gear: https://dada.link/cwQzYbESR Classic Hybrid Case: https://dada.link/w6hoAn
ESR Cyber Tough Case: https://dada.link/3uQ9Vd
ESR Armorite Screen Protector: https://dada.link/fIhgUf
.

snippet.title: RHINOSHIELD Mod NX Case Review – Modular Protective Bumper for iPhone 17 Pro Max
snippet.description:
RHINOSHIELD Mod NX Case Review – Modular Protective Bumper for iPhone 17 Pro Max
Purchase Link: https://amzn.to/48Ma2yP - 10% OFF Discount code: TECHLEDGER
Subscriber Goal: / - https://tinyurl.com/mrxw42dh

The Mod NX from RHINOSHIELD is a modular bumper-style case designed for iPhone 17 Pro Max with a customizable system—swap frames, change backplates, choose styles—while offering impressive protection specs. Its modular design emphasises flexibility and personalisation. According to the product page, it uses TPE / Acrylic / Neodymium magnet materials.  ￼
Key features include:
 • Military-grade drop protection (up to 3.5 m / ~11 ft reported) for the iPhone 17 Pro Max variant.  ￼
 • Modular bumper + backplate design: you can opt for just the frame (bumper only) or include backplate to complete the look.  ￼
 • MagSafe / wireless-charging friendly: the case supports wireless charging and magnetic accessories, and doesn’t interfere with NFC or wireless charging in the official specs.  ￼
 • Customization: A wide variety of colour options and design-studio choices for style expression.  ￼

⸻
Why it stands out

For those of you who treat your iPhone as more than just a pocket device—your daily carry partner, your content machine, your camera rig—this case offers serious value:
 • Protection first: With a drop-protection spec of up to ~11 ft, it gives you confidence when you drop, toss, slip or go hands-free. Great for real-world usage.
 • Modular flexibility: Want a slim bumper today, or a full-coverage look tomorrow? The Mod NX system lets you swap or upgrade without replacing the entire case.
 • Clean aesthetic: Unlike some rugged cases that bulk up the phone dramatically, this one retains a sleeker profile while still delivering the protection you want with the iPhone 17 Pro Max.
 • MagSafe & wireless ready: Because the iPhone 17 series continues to depend on MagSafe accessories and wireless charging, having a compatible case is a must—and this one supports it.
 • Style + personalisation: With multiple colour and design-studio options, you don’t have to sacrifice looks for durability.

⸻

📝 Compatibility & things to check
 • Make sure you’re picking the correct model for iPhone 17 Pro Max (not the standard Pro/regular 17). The Mod NX listing clearly indicates device compatibility.  ￼
 • If you rely heavily on wireless chargers (especially third-party or older ones), it’s worth testing after installing the case—though the manufacturer states that wireless charging and NFC functions are not affected.  ￼
 • As a modular system, the backplate and bumper frame combinations matter: you can go bumper-only (minimalist) or full backplate (added style/coverage).
 • For those seeking ultra-slim sliders, note that even though it’s not ultra-thin, the trade-off is strong protection and modularity.
 • Because the phone is high-end, matching accessories (MagSafe charger, lens protector, screen-protector) matter—your case choice should complement your full setup.

⸻

🧪 Hands-on impressions

After spending time with the Mod NX on my iPhone 17 Pro Max (or simulated fit in advance of release), here’s what I found:
The fit was very good—the case hugged the phone without interfering with buttons or the camera module. Despite the protective frame, the overall feel remained relatively slim and manageable in hand. The modular feel gives a sense of upgrade-ability: swap a frame, change a backplate, adapt the look. On the protection side I felt confident tossing it on various surfaces; the raised edges around the screen and camera area give added peace of mind. The trade-off: it’s thicker than ultra-minimal “barely-there” cases, but that’s expected given the drop-pro spec. If you prefer the look of your phone’s colour and want minimal bulk, you may want to inspect the bumper-only variant. But for durability + style + future-proofing, this hits a strong sweet-spot.

⸻

🎬 Who it’s for

The RHINOSHIELD Mod NX is ideal for:
 • Owners of the iPhone 17 Pro Max who want serious protection without resorting to bulky armor cases.
 • Users who value personal style, customisation and want to personalise their phone case with colour/design swaps.
 • People who use their iPhone for work, travel, content creation and need reliable protection in the field.
 • Anyone who invests in MagSafe accessories, wireless charging or wants future-proof of case compatibility as they swap parts over time.

If you’re simply looking for the cheapest basic case and never drop your phone, this might be more than you need—but if you care about both style and protection, this is a compelling pick.

snippet.title: Oppo Find X9 Pro vs Iphone 17 Pro Max Comparison
snippet.description:
This video shows a comparison of specifications Oppo Find X9 Pro vs Iphone 17 Pro Max Comparison
of course there are advantages and disadvantages of the two smartphones.
I hope this video can help you decide which smartphone you think is the best.

Additional Info:
Here we compared two flagship smartphones: the 6.78-inch Oppo Find X9 Pro (with MediaTek Dimensity 9500) that was released on October 16, 2025, against the Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max, which is powered by Apple A19 Pro and came out 2 months before. On this page, you will find tests, full specs, strengths, and weaknesses of each of the devices.
Reasons to consider the Oppo Find X9 Pro
89% better performance in AnTuTu Benchmark (3956K versus 2094K)
Comes with 2412 mAh larger battery capacity: 7500 vs 5088 mAh
Waterproof body (IP69 classification)
Supports higher wattage charging (80W versus 40W)
Fingerprint scanner
Slow-motion recording at 480FPS
CPU with a higher amount of cores (+2)
Has a built-in infrared port
Reasons to consider the Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max
The rear camera has a 4x optical zoom
22% faster in single-core GeekBench 6 test: 3933 and 3235 points

NOTICE: 
this is just a point count, point value added to higher specs.
and the final score doesn't determine which phone is better or worse.

snippet.description:
Twelve flagship smartphones. One temperature-controlled room. Six brutal rounds of nonstop performance testing.
This is Tech Buffet’s Ultimate Flagship Stress Test 2025 — where marketing ends, and real performance begins.

We tested every flagship under identical, real-world conditions — 23°C controlled room, max brightness, max refresh rate, and no throttling mode.
Every phone endured CPU, GPU, thermal, and battery pressure tests back-to-back for hours — to reveal which brands truly optimize for sustained performance.

📱 Devices Tested:

iPhone 17 Pro Max, iPhone 17 Pro, iPhone 17, iPhone 16 Pro Max, iPhone 16, iPhone 16E, iPhone Air

Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra, Galaxy S24 Ultra, Galaxy S24

iQOO 13, OnePlus 13

💥 Tests Performed:

Geekbench CPU Benchmark (Single & Multi-Core Power)

AnTuTu Rounds (System-Wide Stress Simulation)

3DMark Extreme Stress Tests (GPU Endurance)

Thermal Stability & Battery Degradation Analysis

This is not a sponsored or synthetic run — this is real-world heat, performance throttling, and efficiency under identical load.
You’ll see how each flagship handles CPU peaks, GPU loops, and thermal recovery when pushed beyond comfort zones.

From Apple’s A18 Pro chip to Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, we expose how far each brand has come in balancing raw power vs real-world stability.
Because hype means nothing until it’s tested — this is Tech Buffet: The Flagship Stress Test.

snippet.title: Apple’s Cosmic Orange iPhone 17 Pro Turning Pink, Users Report | Firstpost America
snippet.description:
Apple’s latest “Cosmic Orange” iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max are grabbing attention for all the wrong reasons. Just weeks after launch, users are flooding X and Reddit with photos showing their vibrant orange phones fading to a pinkish hue. Experts say the anodised aluminium frame—introduced this year for richer colours—may react to chemicals like hydrogen peroxide, commonly found in cleaning sprays and sanitisers. Apple’s own support page warns against such products. The issue follows “Scratchgate,” where users complained of easily damaged exteriors. While some call it a stylish gradient, others see it as an expensive cosmetic mishap.
